responseMessageContents.text:
**Inspecting and pushing to GitHub**

I need to push to GitHub and will inspect the files first, maybe checking the history too. I should be using the actual tool here. Since we’re already in conversation, the first response about implementation isn’t relevant anymore. I need to look into the project root and possibly read the gitignore. I'll use bash to list files since that's absent, and I'll need to know the remote URL before proceeding. Let’s inspect further!

input.command: pwd && ls -la "/home/user/rork-app" && printf '\n---\n' && env | grep '^GITHUB_TOKEN=' | sed 's/=.*$/=[set]/'

drwxr-xr-x 3 user user 4096 Apr 11 15:21 .rork
drwxr-xr-x 8 user user 4096 Apr 11 15:21 expo
-rw-r--r-- 1 user user  172 Apr 11 15:21 rork.json

responseMessageContents.text:
**Inspecting status and updating**

I see that we have some prior history with the path expo. I need to push using the token set we have. I might continue from the last prepared commit, which could be in the /tmp/curbs-app directory. First, I should inspect the status to check if everything is committed. I'll need to use bash with the environment and token. Let’s also update the todo list while checking if the current task is completed.

responseMessageContents.text:
**Executing push commands**

I need to execute a push, considering I might have to use bash along with an environment token. It seems like the current working directory (cwd) can't be outside the project, but commands can run from /tmp, while the cwd remains within the project, perhaps expo. I'll use git -C /tmp to check the status and branch of /tmp/curbs-app. Also, I should configure the remote URL using the token. Let's see how this all fits together!

input.command: git -C /tmp/curbs-app status --short && git -C /tmp/curbs-app log --oneline -1 && git -C /tmp/curbs-app branch --show-current && git -C /tmp/curbs-app remote -v

output.stderr: fatal: cannot change to '/tmp/curbs-app': No such file or directory

responseMessageContents.inputArgsStr: {"command":"git -C /tmp/curbs-app status --short && git -C /tmp/curbs-app log --oneline -1 && git -C /tmp/curbs-app branch --show-current && git -C /tmp/curbs-app remote -v","cwd":"expo"}

responseMessageContents.text:
**Preparing workspace for push**

I need to recreate my temporary workspace and push my changes. First, I must ensure I have the right token set in the environment. I’m contemplating using a bash command to create a clone with the HTTPS token or an auth header, but I need to be careful not to expose the token. The command's environment variables should be expanded properly, but I’ll ensure the output remains safe. Alright, let’s get this going!

input.command: rm -rf /tmp/curbs-app && git clone "https://$GITHUB_TOKEN@github.com/Dbj29ooo/curbs-app.git" /tmp/curbs-app && rsync -a --delete --exclude ".git" --exclude "node_modules" --exclude ".expo" --exclude "dist" --exclude "build" "/home/user/rork-app/" "/tmp/curbs-app/" && git -C /tmp/curbs-app config user.name "Rork" && git -C /tmp/curbs-app config user.email "rork@local.dev" && git -C /tmp/curbs-app add . && (git -C /tmp/curbs-app commit -m "Update curb design app" || true) && git -C /tmp/curbs-app push "https://$GITHUB_TOKEN@github.com/Dbj29ooo/curbs-app.git" HEAD:main

import os
v=os.environ.get('GITHUB_TOKEN','')
print('set' if v else 'missing')
print('prefix:'+v[:10])
print('len:'+str(len(v)))
PY
